appliedUpdates.description: Added install-scoping quirk that project-scope skills dry-run may show up-to-date without explicit per-file destination lines and guidance on pairing with user-scope/path-emitting commands for evidence.

frictions.description: With AF_SKIP_RULESYNC_EXEC=1, `skills -y -n` produced no DRY-RUN destination lines, so strict scope destination evidence for skills required two additional dry-run checks without AF_SKIP_RULESYNC_EXEC.
frictions.resolution: Re-ran `skills -y -n` and `skills -y -n --user` (still dry-run) to capture concrete destination paths under HOME for --user scope.

frictions.description: `hooks` family has no installable .sh artifacts in this repo, so both scopes return informational no-hook messages instead of destination DRY-RUN lines.
frictions.resolution: Used deterministic exit codes/messages for hooks and relied on rules/subagents/skills scope evidence for destination routing checks.

frictions.description: rulesync output includes ANSI color/control sequences, making raw substring checks noisy.
frictions.resolution: Validated assertions using stable plain-text substrings (Detected languages, DRY RUN paths, warning text).

frictions.description: npx/rulesync created npm cache/log files under isolated HOME even with dry-run.
frictions.resolution: Kept HOME fully isolated at /tmp/af-utv-install-scoping-u2/home, so no shared-user contamination.

frictions.description: Project-scope skills dry-run can report 'All files are up to date' without per-file path lines when destination state is already in sync.
frictions.resolution: Used --user dry-run variants for explicit path evidence where needed while keeping the required project-scope checks for scope assertions.
